steps.title: Clear the denominator
steps.content: We start with $$f(x)=\frac{9}{7}x+\frac{8}{7}=5$$. Multiply both sides by 7 to eliminate fractions: $$7\cdot\left(\frac{9}{7}x+\frac{8}{7}\right)=7\cdot5$$. Simplify: $$9x+8=35$$.

steps.title: Solve for x
steps.content: From $$9x+8=35$$, subtract 8: $$9x=27$$. Divide by 9: $$x=3$$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
